title: A Big Problem for the Prime Minister
summary: This article talks about a big problem for the prime minister, Sir Keir Starmer. He sent Lord Mandelson to Washington, but there was trouble because Lord Mandelson failed a security check. The Foreign Office overruled this, and it caused a lot of upset. Sir Olly Robbins, a top civil servant, lost his job because of this. People in the opposition and even in the Labour Party are angry and think the prime minister might have to resign. The prime minister says he didn't know about the security check failure until later. He will talk to Parliament about it on Monday. Sir Keir is very angry, and many people say they didn't know what was happening. This story shows how mistakes can cause big problems in government.

keywords.explanation: a test to make sure someone is safe for a job
the part of government that deals with other countries
the group that makes laws in the country
a person who works for the government

questions.question: Who is the prime minister in this story?
Who lost their job because of this problem?
What did the Foreign Office do about the security check?
When will the prime minister talk to Parliament?
How does Sir Keir feel about this problem?

background_read: In the UK, the prime minister is the head of the government and makes important decisions. Sometimes, people in government need to pass security checks to do certain jobs, like working with other countries. If there is a problem, it can cause big arguments, and people might lose their jobs or have to resign. Parliament is where politicians discuss laws and issues, and they often ask the prime minister to explain what happened.
Article_Structure: WHO: Sir Keir Starmer, the prime minister, and Lord Mandelson, who was sent to Washington.
WHAT: A problem where Lord Mandelson failed a security check, but the Foreign Office overruled it, causing job losses and anger.
WHERE: In the UK government and Parliament.
WHY: Because mistakes in government decisions can lead to big consequences, like people losing jobs or the prime minister facing pressure to resign.

perspectives.description: The prime minister says he didn't know about the security check failure until later and will explain to Parliament.
Opposition parties think the prime minister misled people and might have to resign if he knew about the problem.
